item.title: Courage can be difficult at times
item.description: Great guy. Best friend for several years, and first relationship after an abusive relationship. He was really patient with me, but eventually I realized he was using a tactic my ex had used as a tool to abuse me. I pointed it out, asked him to stop, and explained why and how it made me feel... but he kept doing it despite me asking multiple times. Eventually he said that it was just how he is. The easy thing would have been to stay in the relationship and just deal with it... I had done that with my ex, and I should have seen the red flags sooner for what they were before wasting a decade of my life to unhappiness. I'm proud of myself for being able to draw the line this time. I will miss him. He is a great guy. But I also need to stand up for me, even if that means speaking up against people I care for. Your own voice matters too. Especially when it's screaming out inside when things seem to be otherwise fine.
item.dc:creator: Canhealerous

item.title: This is how democracy dies
item.description: This is the one that will end us. The case, Moore v. Harper, challenges a decision by the North Carolina Supreme Court invalidating the maps of the state’s GOP-dominated General Assembly for egregious partisan gerrymandering. Legislators, led by Rep. Tim Moore (R), argued that their branch of government has the power of conducting “Times, Places and Manner of holding elections” under the U.S. Constitution, a notion commonly known as the independent state legislature theory. The challengers’ case posits that this power trumps that of the courts to decide that their actions violate state constitutions or results in unfair maps. Essentially, it gives legislators unchecked power over elections, allowing whoever is in power to set the rules forever in their own favor. Which is exactly the point. Mallory McMorrow (MI state senator) explains further: "Taking up Moore v. Harper gives the U.S. Supreme Court the opportunity to apply a doctrine supported by the Court's new extreme conservative majority called “independent state legislature theory,” or ISLT. ISLT attempts to vest all control of elections in the hands of only the state legislature, letting them throw out the decisions of state courts, redistricting commissions, Secretaries of State, and even state constitutions. If the Court applies this theory, because Republicans control both legislative houses in 30 states, including swing states like here in Michigan, it allows them — and only them — the ability to choose how an election is decided, run, and what districts should look like. If the Supreme Court enshrines ISLT, it means Trump's Republican Party won't need a coup after the election to install him as President in 2024, they can just change the rules in swing states ahead of time to ensure they get the outcome they want." This will end 6-3, like every other atrocious ruling under this court. We'll never have another January 6th because we'll never need to. EDIT TO ADD: VOTE. THIS. MIDTERM. I don't want to hear "voting doesn't matter." This may be the LAST election that it actually matters.
item.dc:creator: MegMakesPuns

item.dc:creator: springelmogizmo

item.title: Remembering Alan
item.description: A final thought for Pride Month 2022. This is Alan. Alan Turing ended his own life on 7th June 1954. He laced an apple with cyanide and ate a bite from it. He did this because the British government chemically castrated, humiliated, and prosecuted him for being gay. Ok, so what did he do? He invented computer science and using his first designs he decrypted the Enigma code - the machine base encryption that the Nazis and the German military used to communicate secret commands to each other in world war 2 - and hence he both saved millions of lives and brought us into the modern age of computing. Pride month isn’t just about dancing on floats in gold hot pants or flying a rainbow flag. It’s about remembering that everyone has a right to be happy, love who they want to love, and recognize the outstanding contribution that everyone can make in a society free of fear or prejudice. Remembering Alan…..
item.dc:creator: mfrybeasley

item.title: D&D Ukraine
item.description: ми отримали вас, родина. Is how to say "We got you, fam" in Ukrainian. Someone told me a better translation is: "я завжди з вами" (I'm always with you) or "я вас підтримую" (I support you) I still say this in English every day: please end this war now.
item.dc:creator: cryborg

item.title: When the scritches are juuuuuust right
item.description: This boy came to us from a case of over 120 cats in 2 trailers. Most are quite undersocialized and scared, this boy is a bit different FP edit: look at that! Well, while you're here... Black lives matter Trans rights are human rights Fuck all nazis The right to abortion is the right to privacy And, please, do something kind for someone
